DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT

Defendant's demurrer to the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is sustained without leave to amend as to causes of action seven [Labor Code section 98.6] and eight [Labor Code section 1102.5] and overruled as to the remainder. (CCP 430.10(e).)

(see word minutes for complete ruling)

Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Off Calendar

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-19-2025 Continued To Mar-05-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Opposing party did not provide a courtesy copy of its opposition papers as required by SFLR 2.7(B). The matter is continued for hearing to March 5, 2025. Opposing party is ordered to provide courtesy copies forthwith.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-31-2024 Continued To Mar-19-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant's demurrer to all nine causes of action in the complaint is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. CCP 425.10(a)(1) requires that a complaint "shall contain [a] statement of the facts constituting the cause of action, in ordinary and concise language." Plaintiff's complaint is totally conclusory and does not plead ultimate facts to support any of the causes of action. Although plaintiff alleges that defendant(s) engaged in discrimination, harassment, retaliation, and other illegal conduct, his complaint does not plead any ultimate facts regarding the employment relationship, plaintiff's alleged protected activity, workplace safety violations, or essential matters The complaint does not even clearly allege that moving defendant was plaintiff's employer. Plaintiff has leave to amend to plead facts to support his claims.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT OPEN TOP SIGHTSEEING SAN FRANCISCO, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendant's motion to strike portions of plaintiff's complaint, which is unopposed, is off calendar as moot in light of the ruling on the demurrer.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)
